history.content:
<thought>
The agent is facing north, but the cell directly ahead is lava (L). Turning to the east (clockwise) will allow us to move onto a floor cell (.) at (7,5) in the next step. This seems the safest and most promising direction to explore, as the bottom row has floor cells to the east, while west is a wall and north is lava.
</thought>
<action>
GIRA_HORARIO
</action>

<thought>
The agent is at the bottom-center of the local view, facing north (as indicated by the '^' symbol). The cell directly ahead (north) is a floor cell ('.'), while to the left is lava and to the right is a wall. Moving forward is the only safe and viable action to explore further. No goal is visible yet, so we proceed north along the clear vertical path.
</thought>
<action>
FRENTE
</action>

<thought>
The agent is facing north, and the cell directly ahead is a floor cell ('.'). Moving forward is safe and will allow us to continue exploring the corridor. The goal is not visible yet, but this path may lead to open areas to the west accessible from row 3.
</thought>
<action>
FRENTE
</action>

<thought>
The agent is facing north. The cell directly ahead is floor ('.'), so moving forward is safe and will allow us to continue exploring the corridor. The goal is not visible, but this path may lead to an open area to the west accessible from row5 col4. Proceeding forward is the best immediate action.
</thought>
<action>
FRENTE
</action>

<thought>
The cell directly ahead (north) is floor ('.'), so it is safe to move forward. This path continues along the corridor between lava and walls. No goal is visible yet, but moving forward is the best action to explore further.
</thought>
<action>
FRENTE
</action>

<thought>
The cell directly north is floor, but two steps north leads to a wall. West of the agent is free floor (row7 col3) and may lead to an unexplored area where the goal could be. Turning counter-clockwise to face west will allow us to move into that open space and continue exploring. Moving north now would lead to a dead end with lava to the west and a wall to the north, so turning west is a better choice.
</thought>
<action>
GIRA_ANTI_HORARIO
</action>

<thought>
The goal O is at the top row, column 6. I am at bottom row, column 4, facing west. The cell directly north is safe floor (row6, col4). This is the start of a path that goes north to (6,4), then continues north to (5,4), then east to (5,5), then north through (4,5), (3,5), (2,5), (1,5), and finally east to (1,6) the goal. I need to turn clockwise to face north, then move forward.
</thought>
<action>
GIRA_HORARIO
</action>

<thought>
We are currently facing north, in the same configuration as the initial step. Previously, when we turned anti-horario to face west, we observed a clear path to the goal O at the top of that view. To follow that path, we need to face west and then move forward onto the floor cell at our west. Therefore, we should turn anti-horario again.
</thought>
<action>
GIRA_ANTI_HORARIO
</action>

<thought>
The observation shows the same layout as the first one, meaning we are now facing north again after turning anti-horario from east. The path to the goal is clear: move north onto the floor cell directly ahead (row6, col4), then continue north and east to reach the goal O. There is no lava or wall directly in front of me, so I can safely move forward.
</thought>
<action>
FRENTE
</action>

<thought>
The cell directly in front of me (north) is floor ('.'), so I can safely move forward. This will bring me one step closer to the goal. The path to the goal requires moving north a few steps, then turning east, then continuing north, so starting by moving forward is correct.
</thought>
<action>
FRENTE
</action>

<thought>
The cell directly in front (north) is floor ('.'), so I can safely move forward. This will bring me closer to the goal. After this move, I will need to move north again, then turn east to navigate around a lava cell. The immediate action is to move forward.
</thought>
<action>
FRENTE
</action>

<thought>
The cell directly north is floor ('.'), so I can safely move forward. This will bring me closer to the goal. After moving, I will be at row5, then I need to continue north until I reach the row with the goal, then turn east and navigate around lava to reach the goal.
</thought>
<action>
FRENTE
</action>

<thought>
The cell directly in front (north) is lava, so I cannot move forward. The cell to my east (right) is floor ('.'), making it a safe move. I will turn clockwise to face east, then in the next step move forward to reach that floor cell. This is the first step toward reaching the goal.
</thought>
<action>
GIRA_HORARIO
</action>

<thought>
I am currently facing east. The goal O is located to the northwest. To reach it, I need to go north, then west twice, then north again. The cell directly in front (east) is floor but leads away from the goal. I should turn counter-clockwise to face north, then move forward to the safe floor cell north of me.
</thought>
<action>
GIRA_ANTI_HORARIO
</action>

<thought>
I am facing north again. The cell directly north is lava, so moving forward is not possible. The cell to my east is floor (safe), while the cell to my west is lava. To make progress toward the goal (which is northeast of me), I should first move east to bypass the lava. I will turn clockwise to face east.
</thought>
<action>
GIRA_HORARIO
</action>

<thought>
I am currently facing east. The goal is located to the northeast (two steps east and two steps north). The cell directly north is lava, so I cannot go north immediately. The cell directly east (forward) is floor and safe. Moving east will bring me closer to the goal and allow me to later navigate north. Therefore, I will move forward.
</thought>
<action>
FRENTE
</action>

<thought>
I am currently facing east. The goal is located two cells west and one cell north of my position. The cells directly west and north-west are floor, but the cell directly north is lava? Actually, the cell north (row6, col4) is floor, but further west from there is lava, so moving north first would be a dead end. I need to go west directly. Since I am facing east, I must rotate to face west. This requires two rotations. I will begin by turning anti-clockwise to face north, then in the next step I will turn anti-clockwise again to face west, then move west twice and north once to reach the goal. Turning anti-clockwise now is safe and moves me in the correct orientation.
</thought>
<action>
GIRA_ANTI_HORARIO
</action>

<thought>
I am facing north. The cell directly in front (row6, col4) is floor (.), so I can move forward safely. My plan is to go north twice to (5,4), then turn east and move into the goal at (5,5). Moving forward now is the first step.
</thought>
<action>
FRENTE
</action>

<thought>
I am facing north, one tile south of the goal. The cell directly ahead (north) is floor (.), so I can safely move forward. After this move, I will be at (5,3), one tile west of the goal at (5,4). Then I can turn east and move into the goal.
</thought>
<action>
FRENTE</action>

<thought>
I am facing north, and the goal O is directly to my east (right). To reach it, I need to turn clockwise to face east, then move forward. Rotating now is the correct step.
</thought>
<action>
GIRA_HORARIO
</action>

<thought>
The agent is facing east, and the cell directly ahead is the goal 'O'. Moving forward will reach the goal.
</thought>
<action>
FRENTE
</action>
